derivation: Push/pop are the LIFO primitives of a stack; a queue uses enqueue/dequeue.
Class A = /8, network ID is the first octet only, so mask 255.0.0.0 (B=/16 Class B, C=/24 Class C).
FDDI is a dual counter-rotating token RING; stem is loose (FDDI is not Ethernet) but Ring is the only defensible option.
Only the physical layer touches the medium, converting the bit stream into electrical/optical/EM signals.
Transport = end-to-end (process-to-process) delivery; node-to-node is data link, routing tables are network layer.

Network virtual terminal, file transfer/access/management and mail service are ALL standard OSI application-layer services, so 'All of the above' is the only self-consistent answer; keying any single one is inconsistent.
FDDI = Fiber Distributed Data Interface, but options A and D are byte-identical, so two labels are simultaneously correct; the item as printed has no unique key.

Only A holds: one CPU executes exactly one process per instant. B is false (multiprogramming preempts/time-slices before completion) and C is false and self-refuting (a single CPU is precisely what needs scheduling), so 'All of the above' cannot stand.
Blocked/waiting holds processes awaiting an external event, typically I/O; processes waiting only for the CPU are in Ready, not Blocked.
States are new/ready/running/blocked/terminated; 'Privileged' is a CPU execution mode, not a process state.
Moore output = f(current state) only; Mealy output = f(current state, current input) = option A.
Running several programs apparently simultaneously is multitasking; the other three are not OS terms for this.
Big-O is the asymptotic upper bound, conventionally used to state the worst case; Omega = lower/best, Theta = tight.
F = x + y'z: x=1 forces rows 100/101/110/111 to 1; y'z=1 only when y=0,z=1, adding row 001; rows 000, 010, 011 are 0.

Read the PNG and traced it: three 3-input ANDs into one OR. Top AND = x1'(inverter on x1) . x2 . x3'(inverter on x3); middle AND = x1 . x2 . x3'; bottom AND = x1 . x2 . x3. So f = x1'x2x3' + x1x2x3' + x1x2x3 = x2(x1 + x3') = minterms 2, 6, 7 -> 1 only at 010, 110, 111.
